hypothesis: Incorporating episodic memory of past simulation failures and force field extraction errors into the multi-agent framework—such that agents can retrieve and condition on structured records of previously encountered parameter misassignment patterns (e.g., unconventional table layouts like those causing EPM2 extraction errors)—will significantly reduce wrong-parameter rates during force field extraction compared to agents operating without such memory, as measured by IoU scores across a diverse benchmark of force field publications with varying table formats.
